title: U.S. bill would tighten scrutiny on PFAS, BPA and phthalates in food packaging
summary: A new No Toxics in Food Packaging Act proposal would target several chemical classes used in food packaging and processing materials, including PFAS, bisphenols and ortho-phthalates. Even before passage, it signals that large buyers will keep asking for clearer chemical disclosures.
buyerTakeaway: For greaseproof paper, cupcake liners and bakery wraps, request PFAS, BPA and phthalate declarations before sampling so compliance questions do not delay retail or foodservice approval.
imageAlt: Food packaging regulation news image from Packaging Dive

title: Taller cupcake liners gain attention as a simple bakery-style presentation upgrade
summary: Consumer baking coverage is highlighting taller paper cupcake liners as a low-cost way to improve cupcake height, shape and shelf appeal. For wholesalers, the detail matters because liner height, rigidity and fit can change the finished look of the baked product.
buyerTakeaway: When sourcing cake cups, specify exact height, base diameter, paper weight and pan compatibility instead of ordering by generic 'standard' size only.
imageAlt: Cupcake liner news image from Good Housekeeping

title: Paper and board suppliers prepare EUDR traceability data for December 2026
summary: Holmen says its paper and board systems are already connected to due diligence statement workflows, with customer reference data available during the transition period. This shows how EUDR is moving from legal text into day-to-day supplier documentation.
buyerTakeaway: For EU-bound paper packaging, ask whether the supplier can provide EUDR reference data or explain why the exact packaging format is outside scope.
imageAlt: EUDR paper origin news image from Holmen Board and Paper

title: BfR consultation puts paper and board food-contact recommendations back under review
summary: Germany's BfR consultation on Recommendation XXXVI covers paper and cardboard materials for food contact and remains influential because paper lacks a single harmonized EU measure. Buyers should expect suppliers to track substance listings and CAS-number details more carefully.
buyerTakeaway: For LFGB/BfR projects, confirm the exact recommendation used for baking paper, cooking paper or paperboard and keep the test report linked to the final grade and coating.

title: Updated EuPIA guidance reinforces documentation needs for food-contact printing inks
summary: EuPIA's April 2026 update highlights good manufacturing practice, risk assessment and supply-chain information for inks used on food contact materials. Printed greaseproof paper and cupcake liners need ink controls as well as paper compliance.
buyerTakeaway: For custom printed food paper, request low-odor ink details, migration support and a statement covering set-off risk, not only the base paper certificate.

title: Air fryer paper liners remain a repeat-purchase consumable through 2035
summary: IndexBox expects air fryer paper liner demand to stay tied to appliance adoption, convenience cooking and commercial kitchen use. The market is also moving toward better sizing, stronger grease resistance, compostability claims and private-label formats.
buyerTakeaway: Retail and foodservice buyers should confirm liner shape, perforation, heat rating, oil resistance, warning labels and carton packing before scaling orders.
imageAlt: Air fryer paper liners market image from IndexBox

title: EU PPWR PFAS limits make test method quality a buyer approval issue
summary: Food Safety Magazine notes that from 12 August 2026, EU food-contact packaging must meet PPWR PFAS limit values and that defensible lab data matters near the thresholds. For paper packaging, this affects coatings, barriers and converted products.
buyerTakeaway: Ask for PFAS test reports with method details and confirm whether the report covers the finished printed or coated item, not only raw paper.
imageAlt: Food packaging image from Food Safety Magazine PFAS article

title: EUDR forces paper, pulp and printing buyers to organize origin data earlier
summary: EUDR preparation is pushing the paper, pulp and printing supply chain toward better raw-material origin records, certificate management and supplier due diligence. Manual document collection becomes risky as deadlines approach.
buyerTakeaway: For European launches, include EUDR document readiness in the sourcing checklist before approving artwork, price and shipment schedule.
imageAlt: EUDR paper pulp and printing industry news image from osapiens

title: Baking and cooking paper forecast supports long-term bakery packaging demand
summary: Future Market Insights projects baking and cooking paper growth from 2026 to 2036, with bakery use, pre-cut formats and foodservice distribution among the important demand drivers. Buyers are comparing both performance and compliance as volume grows.
buyerTakeaway: Plan annual pricing and stock around repeat-use formats such as pre-cut sheets, rolls, cupcake liners and greaseproof wraps rather than treating them as seasonal-only items.
imageAlt: Baking and cooking paper market image from Future Market Insights

title: Food contact paper market growth keeps greaseproof and parchment grades in focus
summary: Fortune Business Insights forecasts steady growth for food contact paper through 2034 across kraft, greaseproof, glassine and parchment paper. Demand is linked to takeaway, bakery, QSR, retail food and sustainability-led material shifts.
buyerTakeaway: Compare suppliers on food-contact documentation, odor control, converting consistency and delivery stability, not unit price alone.
imageAlt: Food contact paper market image from Fortune Business Insights
